title: MLX90614 Non-Contact Infrared Temperature Sensor
content: The MLX90614 is a factory-calibrated infrared thermometer IC that measures object (Ta) and ambient (Tamb) temperatures without contact. It uses a thermopile sensor and onboard DSP, outputs temperature via I²C (SMBus) or PWM at 0.02 °C resolution. Object temperature range: −70 to +380 °C; accuracy ±0.5 °C (0–50 °C object).
extended_content: The MLX90614 combines a silicon thermopile detector with an infrared lens (90° half-angle by default; 35° with MLX90614xCC). The IC reads two channels internally: Ta (ambient) and To1 (object); dual-zone variants also provide To2. I²C address default 0x5A (configurable via EEPROM). Data register 0x07 = To1 (object), 0x06 = Ta, raw 15-bit value. Temperature = (raw × 0.02) − 273.15 °C. 3.3 V or 5 V supply; 4.7 kΩ pull-ups on SDA/SCL. Emissivity default 1.0 (appropriate for human skin, matte surfaces); adjustable in EEPROM (0x04) for metallic surfaces (e.g., 0.95 for oxidised steel). Measurement time: 100 ms for single conversion. Applications: non-contact body temperature, oven monitoring, HVAC duct sensing. Field-of-view: 90° cone; for a 10 cm target diameter, place sensor ≤ 6 cm away. Libraries: Adafruit MLX90614, SparkFun MLX90614. Note: sensor requires >50 ms warm-up after power-on.
source: Melexis MLX90614 Datasheet Rev 003; Melexis Application Note ANA90615
